2603.25517.title: NERO-Net: A Neuroevolutionary Approach for the Design of Adversarially Robust CNNs
2603.25517.paper_summary: Neuroevolution automates the complex task of neural network design but often ignores the inherent adversarial fragility of evolved models which is a barrier to adoption in safety-critical scenarios. While robust training methods have received significant attention, the design of architectures exhibiting intrinsic robustness remains largely unexplored. In this paper, we propose NERO-Net, a neuroevolutionary approach to design convolutional neural networks better equipped to resist adversarial attacks. Our search strategy isolates architectural influence on robustness by avoiding adversarial training during the evolutionary loop. As such, our fitness function promotes candidates that, even trained with standard (non-robust) methods, achieve high post-attack accuracy without sacrificing the accuracy on clean samples. We assess NERO-Net on CIFAR-10 with a specific focus on $L_\infty$-robustness. In particular, the fittest individual emerged from evolutionary search with 33% accuracy against FGSM, used as an efficient estimator for robustness during the search phase, while maintaining 87% clean accuracy. Further standard training of this individual boosted these metrics to 47% adversarial and 93% clean accuracy, suggesting inherent architectural robustness. Adversarial training brings the overall accuracy of the model up to 40% against AutoAttack.
2603.25517.paper_summary_zh: 神經進化自動化了神經網絡設計的複雜任務，但往往忽視了進化模型固有的對抗脆弱性，這成為在安全關鍵場景中採用的障礙。雖然穩健的訓練方法受到了相當多的關注，但展現內在穩健性的架構設計仍然在很大程度上未被探索。在本文中，我們提出了NERO-Net，一種神經進化方法，用於設計更能抵禦對抗攻擊的卷積神經網絡。我們的搜索策略通過在進化循環中避免對抗訓練來隔離架構對穩健性的影響。因此，我們的適應度函數促進那些即使使用標準（非穩健）方法訓練，仍能在不犧牲乾淨樣本準確率的情況下實現高後攻擊準確率的候選者。我們在CIFAR-10上評估NERO-Net，特別關注$L_\infty$-穩健性。具體而言，從進化搜索中產生的最適個體在FGSM攻擊下的準確率為33%，這在搜索階段被用作穩健性的高效估計器，同時保持87%的乾淨準確率。進一步對這個個體進行標準訓練將這些指標提升至47%的對抗準確率和93%的乾淨準確率，這表明了固有的架構穩健性。對抗訓練使模型的整體準確率提升至40%，對抗AutoAttack的效果。
2603.25517.author: Inês Valentim et.al.
2603.25517.authors: Inês Valentim, Nuno Antunes, Nuno Lourenço
